items.description: Small dark mark or foliation indicator at top left corner above text block.
items.scholarly_note: Likely modern or early foliation/quire mark added post-production; Codex Regius received later numbering and annotations during its Danish royal and Icelandic institutional history.

items.description: Diffuse irregular brownish staining, heaviest in lower right quadrant extending from text block edge into margin.
items.scholarly_note: Classic age-related discoloration on calf vellum from handling, moisture, oxidation, and possible grease or dirt accumulation over 750+ years.

items.description: Small isolated dark specks and gradual darkening along the outer edge of the leaf.
items.scholarly_note: Ink flecks (accidental from quill) or later flyspecks; edge darkening from repeated thumbing and exposure when the codex was unbound or loosely bound.

items.description: Narrow gutter with visible rubbing, slight cockling, and minor surface abrasion near the binding edge.
items.scholarly_note: Mechanical wear from centuries of opening and closing; no added marginal text or glosses.

items.description: Multiple letters with retraced or thickened strokes (ascenders, minims, verticals of h/k/l) and occasional overwritten forms within words.
items.scholarly_note: Scribal self-corrections by the main hand during copying; typical of careful but not error-free work in iron-gall ink. No large erasures, expunction dots, or interlinear additions visible.

items.description: Scattered tiny dark spots and occasional small ink blots between lines and near letter forms.
items.scholarly_note: Pen trials or accidental drops from the scribe's quill; a few may be later accretions or localized ink corrosion products.

items.description: Faint ghosting or show-through of letters from the verso visible in interlinear spaces and some blank areas.
items.scholarly_note: Minor bleed-through common on thinner vellum sheets; does not impair legibility of the primary text.

items.description: Fine linear lighter streaks and surface texture variations across the vellum.
items.scholarly_note: Abrasions from use, storage, or original parchment preparation; consistent with hair-side handling.

items.description: Irregular worn fore-edge with small nicks, linear wear, and loss of surface along the natural leaf edge.
items.scholarly_note: Cumulative mechanical damage to the outer margin; no evidence of deliberate trimming or figurative marks.

items.description: Mild overall yellow-brown patina with localized darker patches near the top edge.
items.scholarly_note: Cumulative light exposure, dust, and oxidation of the vellum and residual surface treatments.

scratches: Fine linear abrasions and lighter surface streaks in lower half and right margin.
Edge wear and nicks along right fore-edge and top margin.
Minor cockling and localized creasing, especially lower right.
damage_notes: Typical edge wear and irregularity at top, right, and bottom of the leaf from use and original preparation.
Prominent diffuse staining in lower right and bottom margin; scattered smaller stains elsewhere.
No through-holes, major tears, or losses within the writing area.
Slight variations in iron-gall ink density and minor fading in places; vellum remains largely intact and legible.
summary: Page 49 (Helgakviða Hundingsbana II) is a plain, functional leaf with a dense single-column text block in the main Gothic book hand and narrow, largely empty margins containing no figurative doodles, faces, animals, pen trials, decorative flourishes, or added text. Visible non-textual features are almost entirely physical: age-related diffuse and spotty brownish staining (especially lower right), fine surface scratches and abrasions, edge wear on three sides, scattered small ink flecks, faint verso show-through, and several instances of minor scribal self-correction via retraced or overwritten letters inside the text block. The leaf exhibits the expected condition of a well-prepared but heavily consulted c. 1270 Icelandic calfskin manuscript with no significant marginalia or decoration on this folio.
